title: Pasta: ASSSERTION FAILED in udp_at_sidx (udp_flow.c:35): flow->f.type == FLOW_UDP
body:
### Issue Description

Since I upgraded to Pasta passt-0^20250415 the pasta process will not stay up.  It crashes.

Once the pasta process stops I can no longer reach the containers inside my pod.  I can create the pod and pasta will run but as soon as I start the first container inside the pod, pasta stops.

I know there is another issue similar to this that was recently reported.  So I waited until the latest passt 20250503 was released to see if it fixed the issue for me and it is still there.   As soon as I downgrade to passt 20250320 it works correctly again.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.Use the following command to create a pod:
podman pod create --name ha -p 8123:8123 -p 5683:5683/udp -p 5353:5353/udp -p 3000:3000 -p 10300:10300 --add-host=djkev:host-gateway
2. Use the following command to create my homeassistant container inside the pod:
podman run -d --pod ha \
        --name homeassistant \
        --restart=unless-stopped \
        -e TZ=America/New_York \
        -e PYTHONPATH=/config/deps \
        -v /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro \
        -v /home/djkev/homeassistant:/config \
        -v /home/djkev/homeassistant/media:/media \
        homeassistant/home-assistant:stable



### Describe the results you received

The pasta process tries to start and stops with the following error in the journalct:

May 05 08:54:55 djkev.lan pasta[1914]: ASSERTION FAILED in udp_at_sidx (udp_flow.c:35): flow->f.type == FLOW_UDP
May 05 08:54:55 djkev.lan audit[1914]: SECCOMP auid=1000 uid=1000 gid=1000 ses=1 subj=kernel pid=1914 comm="pasta.avx2" exe="/usr/bin/pasta.avx2" sig=31 arch=c000003e syscall=186 compat=0 ip=0x7f24c0d20107 code=0x80000000
May 05 08:54:55 djkev.lan audit[1914]: ANOM_ABEND auid=1000 uid=1000 gid=1000 ses=1 subj=kernel pid=1914 comm="pasta.avx2" exe="/usr/bin/pasta.avx2" sig=31 res=1

This looks like it's a UDP issue.  So I tried creating the container without forwarding any UDP ports (5683 and 5353) but it still dies with the same error.

If I just create the pod and start it without creating any containers inside it I can see pasta runs with the following options:

/usr/bin/pasta --config-net -t 3000-3000:3000-3000 -t 8123-8123:8123-8123 -t 10300-10300:10300-10300 -u 5353-5353:5353-5353 -u 5683-5683:5683-5683 --dns-forward 169.254.1.1 -T none -U none --no-map-gw --quiet --netns /run/user/1000/netns/netns-a84f61c3-08dd-0195-002a-d7d9e1cb87a1 --map-guest-addr 169.254.1.2

It will stay running if I just start the pod with no containers inside.  But as soon as I use the above command to create my first container pasta stops and logs the above error to journalctl.  I have other containers but just creating the homeassistant is enough to crash pasta.

### Describe the results you expected

I expected pasta to continue running after I create my first container.  With passt 20250320 it does stay running and works as expected when I create the homeassistant container.

### podman info output

```yaml

comments.body:
Possibly related https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25959 (assert here points to different line but who knows)

I suggest you try the latest pasta from today https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25959#issuecomment-2850317382 and see if it still happens there.

cc @sbrivio-rh

Luap99 thanks for the follow up.  But I did try that version (2025_05_03.587980c) and I have the same issue.  I tried it via the Fedora updates-testing repo as it was just published to there I believe yesterday.

@sbrivio-rh I updated to "passt-0^20250503.g587980c-1.fc42.x86_64" and tried the options you suggested.  I noticed it originally starts up two pasta processes when I pass those pasta options to the podman run.  The first one is the forwarding for the pod that I normally see.  The second one is for he new options you had me add.  This time when it crashes the normal pasta stays running but the second one crashes.  Although I think it still logged to the pcap and log files what we needed.  Here are the two pasta process I see start up as soon as I do the "podman run":

/usr/bin/pasta --config-net -t 3000-3000:3000-3000 -t 8123-8123:8123-8123 -t 10300-10300:10300-10300 -u 5353-5353:5353-5353 -u 5683-5683:5683-5683 --dns-forward 169.254.1.1 -T none -U none --no-map-gw --quiet --netns /run/user/1000/netns/netns-c2ea754c-7fda-fac7-33f0-7b05fb4f9dd5 --map-guest-addr 169.254.1.2

/usr/bin/pasta --config-net -d -p /tmp/ha.pcap -l /tmp/ha.log --dns-forward 169.254.1.1 -t none -u none -T none -U none --no-map-gw --netns /run/user/1000/netns/netns-618aa6a3-80b6-29e1-a7f4-999c9f507551 --map-guest-addr 169.254.1.2

Within a second or two the second one disappears.  But even though the first one is still running I still can't connect to any of the ports on the container.

I attached the pcap and log files.  I had to rename the ha.pcap to ha.pcap.txt to allow me to attach it.

It looks like this is the issue:
3.3824:          Flow 2 (UDP flow): Couldn't connect flow socket: Permission denied

[ha.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20051925/ha.log)

[ha.pcap.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20051926/ha.pcap.txt)

> I attached the pcap and log files. I had to rename the ha.pcap to ha.pcap.txt to allow me to attach it.

Thanks a lot.

> It looks like this is the issue: 3.3824: Flow 2 (UDP flow): Couldn't connect flow socket: Permission denied

I think we have two issues, actually:

1. we can't connect() that UDP socket, because, I suppose, of a missing SELinux rule. This is probably the same as https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2363238 (fixed in this release) but, given https://bugs.passt.top/show_bug.cgi?id=81, Podman still runs pasta as `container_runtime_t`, not as `pasta_t`, so while the issue is solved for stand-alone pasta (and passt(1)), we probably need to add an equivalent rule in https://github.com/containers/container-selinux

2. we fail to handle the issue above. This doesn't actually look as critical because, if we can't connect() UDP sockets, we might not be able to do much anyway. But surely this needs to be fixed

I'm looking into 1. now. I didn't reproduce this yet, so, if you're around, the SELinux warning report from /var/log/audit/audit.log (or audit2allow) might save me some time. Regardless of me reproducing it, it would be good to confirm, in any case, where the denial comes from, in your setup.

I actually have selinux turned off (disabled).  Could that be causing an issue?  That being said I do see items in that audit.log.  I will attach it.  But when I run getenforce it returns Disabled.

[audit.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20055838/audit.log)

Seccomp?

$ grep -i seccomp audit.log 
type=SECCOMP msg=audit(1746448685.050:98829): auid=1000 uid=1000 gid=1000 ses=1 subj=kernel pid=235626 comm="pasta.avx2" exe="/usr/bin/pasta.avx2" sig=31 arch=c000003e syscall=186 compat=0 ip=0x7fd9a8a60e47 code=0x80000000AUID="djkev" UID="djkev" GID="djkev" ARCH=x86_64 SYSCALL=gettid
type=SECCOMP msg=audit(1746449187.372:222): auid=1000 uid=1000 gid=1000 ses=1 subj=kernel pid=2030 comm="pasta.avx2" exe="/usr/bin/pasta.avx2" sig=31 arch=c000003e syscall=186 compat=0 ip=0x7f72c3bad107 code=0x80000000AUID="djkev" UID="djkev" GID="djkev" ARCH=x86_64 SYSCALL=gettid
type=SECCOMP msg=audit(1746449310.499:165): auid=1000 uid=1000 gid=1000 ses=1 subj=kernel pid=1917 comm="pasta.avx2" exe="/usr/bin/pasta.avx2" sig=31 arch=c000003e syscall=186 compat=0 ip=0x7f8085134107 code=0x80000000AUID="djkev" UID="djkev" GID="djkev" ARCH=x86_64 SYSCALL=gettid
type=SECCOMP msg=audit(1746449695.112:166): auid=1000 uid=1000 gid=1000 ses=1 subj=kernel pid=1914 comm="pasta.avx2" exe="/usr/bin/pasta.avx2" sig=31 arch=c000003e syscall=186 compat=0 ip=0x7f24c0d20107 code=0x80000000AUID="djkev" UID="djkev" GID="djkev" ARCH=x86_64 SYSCALL=gettid
type=SECCOMP msg=audit(1746457336.131:1437): auid=1000 uid=1000 gid=1000 ses=1 subj=kernel pid=14715 comm="pasta.avx2" exe="/usr/bin/pasta.avx2" sig=31 arch=c000003e syscall=186 compat=0 ip=0x7fddc334b107 code=0x80000000AUID="djkev" UID="djkev" GID="djkev" ARCH=x86_64 SYSCALL=gettid
type=SECCOMP msg=audit(1746457489.689:1454): auid=1000 uid=1000 gid=1000 ses=1 subj=kernel pid=15237 comm="pasta.avx2" exe="/usr/bin/pasta.avx2" sig=31 arch=c000003e syscall=186 compat=0 ip=0x7f67de1de107 code=0x80000000AUID="djkev" UID="djkev" GID="djkev" ARCH=x86_64 SYSCALL=gettid
type=SECCOMP msg=audit(1746457572.593:1470): auid=1000 uid=1000 gid=1000 ses=1 subj=kernel pid=15584 comm="pasta.avx2" exe="/usr/bin/pasta.avx2" sig=31 arch=c000003e syscall=186 compat=0 ip=0x7f220883e107 code=0x80000000AUID="djkev" UID="djkev" GID="djkev" ARCH=x86_64 SYSCALL=gettid
type=SECCOMP msg=audit(1746457848.224:1558): auid=1000 uid=1000 gid=1000 ses=1 subj=kernel pid=16199 comm="pasta.avx2" exe="/usr/bin/pasta.avx2" sig=31 arch=c000003e syscall=186 compat=0 ip=0x7f41b418a107 code=0x80000000AUID="djkev" UID="djkev" GID="djkev" ARCH=x86_64 SYSCALL=gettid
type=SECCOMP msg=audit(1746495378.415:6107): auid=1000 uid=1000 gid=1000 ses=1 subj=kernel pid=32849 comm="pasta.avx2" exe="/usr/bin/pasta.avx2" sig=31 arch=c000003e syscall=186 compat=0 ip=0x7ff3b5cb6107 code=0x80000000AUID="djkev" UID="djkev" GID="djkev" ARCH=x86_64 SYSCALL=gettid
type=SECCOMP msg=audit(1746495521.707:6123): auid=1000 uid=1000 gid=1000 ses=1 subj=kernel pid=33255 comm="pasta.avx2" exe="/usr/bin/pasta.avx2" sig=31 arch=c000003e syscall=186 compat=0 ip=0x7f4c8af2a107 code=0x80000000AUID="djkev" UID="djkev" GID="djkev" ARCH=x86_64 SYSCALL=gettid

https://stopdisablingselinux.com/

> I actually have selinux turned off (disabled). Could that be causing an issue?

No, sorry, that was me jumping to conclusions. It looks like we get EINVAL if we try to bind to a multicast address, but EPERM if we connect() to a broadcast address for whatever reason. I haven't reproduced it yet but the issue looks clear enough for the moment. I'm on it.

> That being said I do see items in that audit.log.

> Seccomp?

...don't mind those. That's gettid() failing on our abort() calls when we terminate on passt's ASSERT(), it's expected, and we don't want to add gettid() and a couple of others to our seccomp profile just because glibc happens to call them on abort().
Should we dontaudit it in seccomp? (If that is possible?) @giuseppe thoughts?

> Should we dontaudit it in seccomp? (If that is possible?) [@giuseppe](https://github.com/giuseppe) thoughts?

the seccomp profile should not matter to pasta, or is podman running inside another container?

We don't use `SECCOMP_FILTER_FLAG_LOG` AFAICS

> Should we dontaudit it in seccomp? (If that is possible?)

It's not possible. It could be done in auditd, but it looks risky. Besides, we shouldn't be calling abort(), so once this is solved, the noise is also gone.

> the seccomp profile should not matter to pasta

This is pasta's own seccomp profile.

@kevinfowler while forwarding broadcast packets from inside the container is not something we properly support at the moment, the failure to connect() to 255.255.255.255 actually seems to be handled properly on my setup, and I can't reproduce the issue, not with the homeassistant/home-assistant:stable container, and not with a plain `socat` command.

I prepared a branch with some debugging changes. Would you be able to try it out? Rebuilding pasta takes just a few seonds, and the whole procedure should take around a minute. You can remove the local build cleanly afterwards (with `sudo make uninstall`).

The instructions are essentially the same as https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25959#issuecomment-2839447682 (partial copy and paste here for your convenience):

    git clone -b podman_26073 git://passt.top/passt && cd passt && make && sudo make install

...then restart your container, and trigger the assertion. Once the problem occurs, some more messages will be printed in the log file, and a core dump will be generated. It can be exported using corectldump(1): list with corectldump list, and then export the last one, for example:

    $ coredumpctl list
    TIME                            PID  UID  GID SIG    COREFILE EXE                             SIZE
    Tue 2025-04-29 15:43:59 UTC 1240504 1001 1001 SIGSYS present  /home/sbrivio/passt/passt.avx2 32.4K
    $ coredumpctl dump 1240504 -o core.dump
    [...]

thanks in advance!

By the way, I can also prepare a package if you prefer that.

So the question is how a valid flow is provided to ``udp_at_sidx()`` with an invalid type?

Some functions call ``flowside_lookup(IPPROTO_UDP)`` that selects the flow. This could return a valid sidx with an invalid type if the hash table is full and the flow is not found (or if the previous hash entry has not been removed correctly ?). 

To check this hypothesis, we should try to add:
```
diff --git a/flow.c b/flow.c
index c5718e3c7bf7..77785444cb92 100644
--- a/flow.c
+++ b/flow.c
@@ -727,6 +727,11 @@ static flow_sidx_t flowside_lookup(const struct ctx *c, uint8_t proto,
                 flowside_eq(&flow->f.side[sidx.sidei], side)))
                b = mod_sub(b, 1, FLOW_HASH_SIZE);
 
+       if (!(FLOW_PROTO(&flow->f) == proto &&
+             flow->f.pif[sidx.sidei] == pif &&
+             flowside_eq(&flow->f.side[sidx.sidei], side)))
+               return FLOW_SIDX_NONE;
+
        return flow_hashtab[b];
 }
 
```

> So the question is how a valid flow is provided to `udp_at_sidx()` with an invalid type?

My main [suspicion](https://archives.passt.top/passt-dev/20250505215519.6d745232@elisabeth/) was about a flow we look up with udp_flow_from_sock() and we get a valid result but somehow it's not a UDP flow. But I don't have any concrete path in mind.

> Some functions call `flowside_lookup(IPPROTO_UDP)` that selects the flow. This could return a valid sidx with an invalid type if the hash table is full and the flow is not found (or if the previous hash entry has not been removed correctly ?).

Unlikely that it's full, though, we just started... :(

> To check this hypothesis, we should try to add:
> [...]

Added to the `podman_26073` branch (with some prints around it).

> > By the way, I can also prepare a package if you prefer that.
> 
> [@sbrivio-rh](https://github.com/sbrivio-rh) I will give that branch a try later today.

Thanks!

> An RPM package would be nice so I don't have to install the source build over top of the RPM that is there now.

Sharing in a moment.

> I actually tried to build an RPM package from that 20250503 branch before one was available and could not get it to work. I was using rpmbuild -ba and the spec file but I was getting an error with the Version line of the spec file. Can you tell me the options you are using to build a package?

Nothing strange, I also use `rpmbuild -ba` when I (occasionally) happen to build the package manually.

But the `passt.spec` file under `contrib/fedora` is an rpkg template, not a spec file ready to use, so we have to pass it through `rpkg spec` first, for example:

```
$ rpkg spec
Wrote: /tmp/rpkg/passt-1-bt1g4pgd/passt.spec
$ sed -i 's/git_hash .*/git_hash 44bd27e6ae9cd1eda6142b197be05abd2dfd7e7a/g' /tmp/rpkg/passt-1-bt1g4pgd/passt.spec
$ spectool -gR /tmp/rpkg/passt-1-bt1g4pgd/passt.spec
Downloading: https://passt.top/passt/snapshot/passt-44bd27e6ae9cd1eda6142b197be05abd2dfd7e7a.tar.xz
| 255.1 KiB Elapsed Time: 0:00:00                                                                                                                                                                                 
Downloaded: passt-44bd27e6ae9cd1eda6142b197be05abd2dfd7e7a.tar.xz
$ rpmbuild -ba /tmp/rpkg/passt-1-bt1g4pgd/passt.spec
[...]
```

> > An RPM package would be nice so I don't have to install the source build over top of the RPM that is there now.
> 
> Sharing in a moment.

[There](https://passt.top/builds/podman_26073/) you go.

I installed the debug versions.  Thanks for the RPMs.  I have attached the log file, pcap, and core dump files.  Also thanks for the explanation on converting that rpkg template.  That makes sense now.

[core.dump.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20073732/core.dump.gz)
[ha.pcap.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20073728/ha.pcap.txt)
[ha.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20073727/ha.log)

Oh, you mentioned not being able to reproduce the issue with the home assistant container.  It's my google home assistants that are using MDNS on port 5353/udp.  I believe when HA reaches out to those via udp port 5353 is when pasta crashes.  This only take a second or two after the container is up.

The weird part is when I first upgrade to passt-2025_04_15.2340bbf, it didn't crash right away but after about 5 minutes or so.  Now if I run 2025_04_15 or 2025_05_03 versions it crashes after 1 or 2 seconds every time.

Hi! :wave: 

Am seeing this on my home server, Gentoo, `passt-2025.04.15`, on Logitech Media Server container. I too run `homeassistant` but have not seen the crash there.

Sorry, I no longer have a development setup. Am just commenting to eliminate Fedora or SELinux as suspects. (I have SELinux, but currently permissive).

According to the coredump, the call is in ``udp_sock_handler()``:
```
902	void udp_sock_handler(const struct ctx *c, union epoll_ref ref,
903			      uint32_t events, const struct timespec *now)
904	{
905		struct udp_flow *uflow = udp_at_sidx(ref.flowside);
```

> Oh, you mentioned not being able to reproduce the issue with the home assistant container. It's my google home assistants that are using MDNS on port 5353/udp. I believe when HA reaches out to those via udp port 5353 is when pasta crashes. This only take a second or two after the container is up.

Right, given the path to the ASSERT() call, that must be it: inbound traffic after "something" happening.

> The weird part is when I first upgrade to passt-2025_04_15.2340bbf, it didn't crash right away but after about 5 minutes or so. Now if I run 2025_04_15 or 2025_05_03 versions it crashes after 1 or 2 seconds every time.

Maybe because whatever external agent needed a while to discover / register your container, the first time it ran.

> Hi! 👋
> 
> Am seeing this on my home server, Gentoo, `passt-2025.04.15`, on Logitech Media Server container. I too run `homeassistant` but have not seen the crash there.

Oh hello there! 👋 It probably depends on some specific inbound traffic.

> According to the coredump, the call is in `udp_sock_handler()`

...and the flow we find is properly freed (type 0, state 0). It's as if we failed to remove a flow from the hash table on a previous failure...

> ..and the flow we find is properly freed (type 0, state 0). It's as if we failed to remove a flow from the hash table on a previous failure...

Or to remove an fd from the epoll_wait() pool, as the sidx comes from an epoll_ref.

``ref`` is
```
{
    { type = EPOLL_TYPE_UDP,
       fd = 147,
       flowside = {
            sidei = 1, 
            flowi = 2
      },
  }
```
``flowi`` provides the flow index and we can see in the traces that it has been released:
```
4.1642:          Flow 2 (UDP flow): Couldn't connect flow socket: Permission denied
4.1643:          Flow 2 (FREE): TYPED -> FREE
4.1643:          Flow 2 (FREE): TAP [192.168.1.25]:50952 -> [255.255.255.255]:1900 => HOST [0.0.0.0]:50952 -> [255.255.255.255]:1900
4.1643:          Dropping datagram with no flow TAP 192.168.1.25:50952 -> 255.255.255.255:1900
```

> We need an `EPOLL_CTL_DEL` in `udp_flow_sock()`.
> 
> Something like that:
> 
> ```
> diff --git a/udp_flow.c b/udp_flow.c
> index bc122404aa28..f15574987d09 100644
> --- a/udp_flow.c
> +++ b/udp_flow.c
> @@ -98,6 +98,8 @@ static int udp_flow_sock(const struct ctx *c,
>  
>         if (flowside_connect(c, s, pif, side) < 0) {
>                 int rc = -errno;
> +
> +               epoll_del(c, s);
>                 flow_dbg_perror(uflow, "Couldn't connect flow socket");
>                 return rc;
>         }
> ```

Thanks, adding to debug branch and making a new build.

There's also the issue, in some sense, that if we look up a flow from a bad reference, we go through flow_at_sidx() -> flow_sidx_valid(), which returns true, and udp_at_sidx() proceeds, even if the flow was freed. Is that flow really "valid"?

Or, in other words, should we actually ASSERT() on flow->f.type != FLOW_UDP? We could potentially ASSERT() on a kernel issue that way. At the same time, we wouldn't have probably not found this issue if we didn't...

I have updated the diff to call epoll_del() only on PIF_HOST, as sock_l4_sa() is only called in this case.

A flow is valid if sidx refers to a valid flow & side:
```
static inline bool flow_sidx_valid(flow_sidx_t sidx)
{
        return sidx.flowi < FLOW_MAX;
}
```
and we create invalid flow with:
```
#define FLOW_SIDX_NONE ((flow_sidx_t){ .flowi = FLOW_MAX })
```
So 'valid' means not 'none'.

ASSERT() is here to find coding bugs and help to debug, it's annoying for user, but if developers are responsive it helps to improve the software. I like ASSERT()...

> I have updated the diff to call epoll_del() only on PIF_HOST, as sock_l4_sa() is only called in this case.

Ah, sure, and regardless of the specific call, it also makes sense conceptually I'd say.

> A flow is valid if sidx refers to a valid flow & side:
> 
> ```
> static inline bool flow_sidx_valid(flow_sidx_t sidx)
> {
>         return sidx.flowi < FLOW_MAX;
> }
> ```
> 
> and we create invalid flow with:
> 
> ```
> #define FLOW_SIDX_NONE ((flow_sidx_t){ .flowi = FLOW_MAX })
> ```
> 
> So 'valid' means not 'none'.

Yeah, that's what I find a bit ambiguous, however it has the advantage that we could trigger the assertion this way.

> ASSERT() is here to find coding bugs and help to debug, it's annoying for user

...and maintainers because now I'll have to make a new release, new packages for Fedora, CentOS Stream, Debian, and on top of that, given the timing, [apply for an unblock](https://release.debian.org/trixie/freeze_policy.html) for trixie.

But fine, I guess it's better than having a stale file descriptor in the epoll list ;)

> but if developers are responsive it helps to improve the software. I like ASSERT()...

Yes yes I get your point. :)

@kevinfowler Did you set the network option on the `podman run` command? When using a pod like that the network option must be set on the `podman pod create` command. By setting on the container you ask for a new private namespace which is not shared with the pod thus the ports would no longer be set and you could not communicate over localhost with the other pod containers.
